top_signals.title: Iran, Oman Say They’ll Work on Pact for Hormuz Transit Costs
top_signals.summary: Iran and Oman said they’ll begin work on finding an agreement over the future administration of the Strait of Hormuz, including the cost of managing transit, as traders and shipowners fret over the prospect of the introduction of a tolling arrangement.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Leveraged ETFs Sold $6 Billion of Korean Chips in Tuesday Rout
top_signals.summary: Leveraged exchange-traded funds tracking Samsung Electronics Co. or SK Hynix Inc. were likely forced to sell a combined $6 billion of the Korean chipmakers’ shares Tuesday to maintain leverage ratios, underscoring how such products can amplify market moves, according to Bloomberg Intelligence.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Oman, Iran Discuss Managing Strait of Hormuz Navigation
top_signals.summary: Oman and Iran agreed on Tuesday to press on with discussions about the future administration of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, including maritime services in the strategic waterway and the costs associated with them.In a joint statement issued after talks in Muscat…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Trump Says Justice Department Looking Into Gasoline Prices
top_signals.summary: US President Donald Trump has ordered the Department of Justice to look into gasoline prices, which he says aren’t falling fast enough.

top_signals.title: Exclusive | Charles Schwab Breaks Into the Prediction Market Business - WSJ
top_signals.summary: Exclusive | Charles Schwab Breaks Into the Prediction Market Business  WSJ
